COUNCIL MEETING
A G E N D A
Tuesday, April 28, 2026 at 5:00 P.m. - 8:00 P.m.
Council Chamber, City Hall
4949 Canada Way, Burnaby, BC

CALL TO ORDER
 
2.
TERRITORIAL ACKNOWLEDGEMENT
 
3.
ANNOUNCEMENTS
 

4.

Open Council Meeting Agenda for April 28, 2026
 
5.
ADOPTION OF MINUTES
 
5.1
Open Council Meeting Minutes April 14, 2026
[image] 
1.2026.04.14 Council Minutes DRAFT.pdf
6.
DELEGATIONS AND INVITED
PRESENTATIONS
 
None.
7.
ADMINISTRATIVE REPORTS
 

7.1
BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL
AND REPLACEMENT
[image] 
1.Burnaby Zoning Bylaw - Proposed Repeal and Replacement
2026-04-28.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026
2026-04-28_.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Proposed Form and Character Development

2026-04-28.pdf

Replacement at Occupancy and Second Tenancy
2026-04-28.pdf

Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning
Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area
Guidelines and zoning district reassignment.

rates.

FMC - BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND

INCORPORATE PROTECTED INTERSECTION

[image]

Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected
intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and
cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility.
9.

CONSENT AGENDA
 
9.1
BRENTWOOD PARK SSMUH MOTION -
RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS AND STATUTORY

[image]

Restrictive Covenants from Provincial SSMUH requirements.
9.2

FROM MARCH 1 - MARCH 31, 2026
[image]

2.Attachment 1 - Building Permit Activity Report No. 03
2026-04-28.pdf

CHC - AS-FOUND REPORT AND STATEMENT OF
SIGNIFICANCE FOR HERITAGE RESOURCES AT

[image]

2.Attachment 1 - Sketch Map (3789 Royal Oak)
2026-04-28.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - As-found report, Forest Lawn Funeral Home

4.Attachment 3 - As-built drawings, Forest Lawn Funeral Home
Chapel (3789 Royal Oak) 2026-04-28.pdf
5.Attachment 4 - Statement of Significance for Forest Lawn

9.4
EC - SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING (SWR) 2025

[image] 
1.EC - Solid Waste and Recycling (SWR) 2025 Annual Report

EC - SU: ENHANCING FOOD SUSTAINABILITY IN

[image]

INERSECTION SAFETY IMPROVEMENTS
[image] 
1.TC - Central Boulevard and Parkade Intersection Safety

Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the proposed

10.
INFORMATION REPORTS
 
None.
11.
BYLAWS
 
11.1
FIRST AND SECOND READING
 
11.1.1
#14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950
Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place)
[image] 
1.14808 Amend No. 7, 2026 REZ 24-23 (Grand
Promenade_Austin Rd_Gatineau PL).ACD 2026.pdf

Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands
pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received
Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two
market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73
(Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail
and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning
application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of
the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and
add 362 additional market strata units from the development
concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional
seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In
addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning
application, the applicant is proposing to provide the
inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be
transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from
the previous rezoning approval. 
(Item 8.2., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026)
11.1.2
#14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway)
[image] 
1.14809 Amendment No. 8, 2026 REZ 25-09 (6535
Kingsway).CD 2026.pdf
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use
multiple-family development with a double-height commercial
storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units,
ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and
underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the
applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations
for the site. 
(Item 8.3., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026)

11.1.3
#14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue)
[image] 
1.14810 Amendment No. 9, 2026 REZ 25-21 (2757 Norland
Ave).CD 2026.pdf
Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed
self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in
accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and
approved transitionary provisions.
(Item 8.2., PDC Report, Council March 24, 2026)
11.2
FIRST, SECOND AND THIRD READING
 
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961,
Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 
[image] 
1.14811.Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961 Amendment
Bylaw No. 1 2026 (E-Bike and Electric Pilot Program).pdf
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-
bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)
(Item 7.5., Administrative Report, Council April 14, 2026)
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979,
Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 

[image] 
1.14812. Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amend Bylaw
No. 1 2026 (E-Bike and Electric Pilot Program).pdf
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979
(e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)
(Item 7.5., Administrative Report, Council April 14, 2026)
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009,
Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 (e-bike and electric kick
scooter programs)
[image] 
1.14813 Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amend Bylaw
No. 3, 2026 BBY Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 .pdf
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement
Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)
(Item 7.5., Administrative Report, Council April 14, 2026)
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
[image] 
1.14814.Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 v2.pdf
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and
improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the
Community Charter
(Item 7.3., Administrative Report, Council April 28, 2026)

Subject to approval of item 7.3. 
12.
CORRESPONDENCE
 
No items of correspondence were received for Council's
decision. All other items of correspondence were published at
the Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions
package dated April 28, 2026.
13.
OTHER BUSINESS 
 
13.1
SCHEDULING: PRCC Meeting
 
Recommendation:
THAT the Parks, Recreation and Culture Committee meeting be
scheduled on Wednesday, May 27, 2026, at 5:00 pm in the
Council Chamber.
14.
RELEASE OF CLOSED MEETING
DECISIONS
 

15.
ADJOURNMENT

briefs.title: Burnaby to Replace Zoning Bylaw
briefs.summary: Burnaby is getting a new zoning bylaw to replace the old one from 1965. A zoning bylaw is a set of rules that control how land can be used and what type of buildings can be built. The council has approved the repeal (ending) and replacement of the old bylaw.
briefs.action_required: Review the new Burnaby zoning bylaw to understand how it affects your business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority Seeks Funding for Operations
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority has asked the City Council for money to run its programs for the coming year. This funding will help the Authority continue to provide housing and support services.

briefs.title: 2026 Property Tax Rates Approved by Council
briefs.summary: The municipal council has approved the property tax rates for 2026. These rates will be used to calculate the amount of property tax that owners will pay for municipal, regional, and Business Improvement Area (BIA) services.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Grocery & Food Retail, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Approving Officer Appointment Requires Council Approval
briefs.summary: A new Approving Officer needs to be appointed. Council approval is now required for this appointment.
briefs.action_required: No action is required. Stay informed about council decisions.

briefs.title: 8012 11th Avenue Retaining Wall Variance Approved
briefs.summary: Council approved Development Variance Permit (DVP) #26-01, concerning retaining walls at 8012 11th Avenue. A Development Variance Permit allows a property owner to vary or deviate from specific requirements in local bylaws.

briefs.title: Insurance Contract Renewals Approved for 2026
briefs.summary: Approval has been granted to award insurance contracts for property and liability programs for 2026. This ensures the continuation of necessary insurance coverage.

briefs.title: Networking Software Licenses Contract Awarded
briefs.summary: A contract for networking software licenses has been awarded. This means the local government has approved spending on these licenses.

briefs.title: New Civic Facility Contract Awarded for Furnishings and Equipment
briefs.summary: A contract has been awarded for the supply of office and facility furniture for a new civic building. This means a specific company will provide these items.

briefs.title: External Audit Contract Change Approved
briefs.summary: A change to the contract for external audit services has been approved. This means the terms of the agreement with the company that checks the business's financial records will be different.
briefs.action_required: Review the changes to the external audit services contract.

briefs.title: Burnaby Lake Geotechnical Contract Change Approved
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby approved a change to a contract for geotechnical (soil and rock) consulting services at the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex. This change involves additional work and costs for the project.

briefs.title: Spring 2026 Community Grant Applications Approved
briefs.summary: The city council has approved the list of recommended groups to receive community grants for Spring 2026. This means these organizations will get funding for their projects.

briefs.title: Burnaby Fees and Charges Bylaw: Mid-Year Fee Changes in 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby is planning to change some fees in its Fees and Charges Bylaw in mid-2026. This bylaw lists the costs for various city services and permits. The exact changes haven't been specified yet, but they will affect businesses that use these services.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about the specific fee changes before mid-2026.

briefs.title: Tenant Protection Framework and Bylaw: Council Endorsement Sought
briefs.summary: A tenant protection framework and bylaw are being considered. If approved, these measures will change the rules for landlords and tenants. The goal is to provide more security and rights for renters.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about the bylaw's progress and potential impact on your rental practices.

briefs.title: Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project Approved
briefs.summary: The city council approved improvements to the Southeast Bikeway. This project aims to enhance the bikeway for all users.

briefs.title: Burnaby to Change Road Design Standards for Protected Intersections
briefs.summary: Burnaby will update its road design rules. The change will include standards for "protected intersection geometry." This refers to how intersections are designed to better protect pedestrians and cyclists.

briefs.title: Burnaby Considers Changes to Property Restrictions in Brentwood Park
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby is looking at ways to change or remove restrictive covenants (agreements that limit how you can use your property) and building schemes (sets of rules about how properties in an area should be developed) in the Brentwood Park area. This could affect what property owners can do with their land.
briefs.action_required: Check City of Burnaby updates to see how this may affect your property.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Burnaby rezoning amended for Grand Promenade, Austin Road
briefs.summary: Burnaby has amended the rezoning for properties located at 3900 Grand Promenade and 6950 Austin Road. This change modifies the previous zoning rules for these specific locations.

briefs.title: Kingsway Development Approved: 12-Storey Mixed-Use Building
briefs.summary: A new bylaw (Bylaw #14809) allows a 12-storey mixed-use building at 6535 Kingsway. This zoning change (REZ #25-09) permits the construction of the development.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Zoning Change for New Self-Storage Building on Norland Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby has approved a change to its zoning rules (Bylaw #14810). This change allows a five-story self-storage building to be built at 2757 Norland Avenue. The change is called Zoning Bylaw Amendment - REZ #25-21.

briefs.title: E-Bike, E-Scooter Pilot Program: Street and Traffic Bylaw Changes
briefs.summary: The city is changing its Street and Traffic Bylaw to allow a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. This means new rules about where and how these devices can be used.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about the pilot program's rules for e-bikes and e-scooters.

briefs.title: Parks Bylaw Amended: E-Bikes and Scooters Pilot Program
briefs.summary: A local bylaw changed to allow a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters in parks. This means there are new rules about where and how these devices can be used in park areas.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated Parks Regulation Bylaw #14812 for e-bike and scooter rules.

briefs.title: E-Bike Program: Bylaw Changes Affecting E-Scooter Enforcement
briefs.summary: The city is changing its rules for enforcing bylaws related to e-bikes and electric kick scooters. This is part of a pilot program to manage these new forms of transportation.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated bylaw to understand the new enforcement rules.

briefs.title: Burnaby Sets 2026 Property Tax Rates
briefs.summary: Burnaby has passed Bylaw #14814 to set the property tax rates for all taxable land and buildings in the city for 2026. This bylaw determines how much property owners will pay in taxes to the city next year.
briefs.action_required: Review your 2026 property tax bill from Burnaby when you receive it.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Construction & Development
